scores.materiality_reasoning: Self-ownership and freedom from enslavement are foundational material resources.
scores.generality_reasoning: Liberty is a core structural axis defining a person's entire legal and economic existence.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Manumission is a lifelong transition from property to personhood.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Slave status is a legal birth-status or structural condition requiring a formal act to change.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Diogenes Laertius quotes the actual legal will of Epicurus from his own era.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The verbatim is a legal dispositive clause in a will, reflecting a factual transfer of rights.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The right to be manumitted via testament is a legal provision, not part of the definition of 'slave'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Maintenance and dowries are direct financial and subsistence resources.
scores.generality_reasoning: The right to inherit and be maintained covers a person's entire economic survival.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Maintenance lasts throughout minority until the age of marriage.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The status of being a minor child of a specific deceased person is biologically fixed.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Relates to contemporary Hellenistic inheritance and guardianship law.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Direct quote from a legal document (will) establishing an enforceable obligation.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a minor does not inherently define one as a beneficiary of Epicurus's specific estate.

scores.materiality_reasoning: A dowry is a tangible transfer of wealth/property.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specifically concerns marital property provision for orphaned daughters.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The dowry once provided typically stays with or protects the woman throughout her marriage and divorce.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Gender and parental status (orphan) are immutable traits.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Relates to Athenian law in the time of the testator.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The verbatim is a legal instruction from a will.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Womanhood does not automatically entail the right to a dowry from a specific benefactor's estate.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Land ownership is a primary form of material wealth and property.
scores.generality_reasoning: Land ownership is a foundational societal right/axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Ownership or exclusion from land is a lifelong structural reality linked to citizenship status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship/foreign status is a hard-to-change legal birthright or status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: The debate concerns the contemporary legal status of Epicurus in Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported as a legal challenge/charge used in a historical dispute.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The definition of 'foreigner' does not universally exclude land ownership in all societies.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to specific pedagogical materials (concise doctrines) for mental health.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to a specific institution (the Garden) and its instructional levels.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Lasts during the 'beginner' phase of a student's education.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Student status 'rank' is acquired through training and can be changed by progress.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the instructional practice of the contemporary Epicurean school.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Factual description of Epicurus's pedagogical method and rationale.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Common education levels do not always mandate specific 'concise' versions of technical texts.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to advanced education with social and professional payoffs.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the curriculm of the Epicurean school.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Advanced knowledge is a durable intellectual and social asset.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Advanced student status is achieved through effortful study.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the target audience for the author's contemporary philosophical works.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes the intended use cases for the text based on student level.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Advanced status does not definitionally include 'detailed natural science' in all schools.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The resource (wisdom) is an abstract spiritual/intellectual trait.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers a broad domain of personal development/ethical life within the school.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Wisdom is framed as a permanent state or unattainable lifelong capacity.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Nationality and bodily constitution are strictly immutable traits.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: The claim is presented as a summary of historical school doctrine.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The verbatim is a dogmatic assertion/opinion about the capacity of certain races.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Nationality does not inherently define one's capacity for wisdom outside this specific doctrine.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Legal restrictions imply constraints on movement, contracts, or property.
scores.generality_reasoning: Submitting to legal restrictions covers a broad range of civil and legal actions.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Legal status based on gender is a lifelong structural reality in this society.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Gender is a biologically fixed, immutable trait.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to contemporary laws of the city known to the author and Diogenes.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Refers to external 'restrictions imposed by the law' as a factual baseline.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Womanhood is not definitionally 'legally restricted'; this is a social rule.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection from corporal punishment is a direct bodily entitlement.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specifically addresses the use of physical punishment within the household.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Leniency based on character is a long-term status but contingent on the master's whim.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Slavery is a hard-to-change structural legal status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the ethical conduct expected in contemporary Epicurean households.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim is an exhortation/ideal proposal ('he will pity them').
scores.tautology_reasoning: Slavery does not inherently exclude the possibility of mercy.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to use courts has direct material and legal consequences.
scores.generality_reasoning: A recurring legal procedural right.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The legal standing to sue is a long-term property of a citizen.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Wise man status is a durable achieved social identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes contemporary legal capabilities of members of the school.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Straightforward description of what the 'wise man' is permitted to do.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The definition of a 'wise man' doesn't necessarily include the right to sue.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to education (philosophy) facilitates long-term human development.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers the entire spectrum of seeking wisdom/education.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Wisdom is described as a lifelong pursuit for the 'health of the soul'.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Age is an immutable biological fact at any given moment.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: A prescriptive core tenet of the contemporary Epicurean school.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The verbatim is a pure exhortation ('Let no one be slow').
scores.tautology_reasoning: Childhood/youth is not definitionally a time for philosophy; many schools restricted it.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Political/royal power and command are concrete leadership resources.
scores.generality_reasoning: Exercise of royalty is a broad category of state power.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Explicitly defined as transient (twelve hours total per term).
scores.immutability_reasoning: Senatorial status was a stable, hereditary-leaning noble status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Plutarch is describing the early mytho-historical Roman interregnum.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes the formal institutional arrangement as an established historical fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Senators are not definitionally revolving kings; this is a specific rule.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The power to select the head of state is a core political resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: Nomination of a monarch is a high-level structural right.
scores.persistence_reasoning: A brief episode/single procedure occurring only during a vacancy.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Patrician factions were rigid hereditary/social classes.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the election of Numa in the legendary Roman past.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Details a specific historical agreement between political elite groups.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a patrician does not inherently include the right to nominate kings.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to a symbolic ceremonial role as a priestly attendant.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to a very specific religious office and ritual procedure.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Bounded to a specific life stage (young boyhood).
scores.immutability_reasoning: Status (age and both parents living) is an immutable biological/life state.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes ancient Roman religious customs from a historian's perspective.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim names the office and criteria as a matter of reported fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Having living parents does not definitionally make one a 'Camillus'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Management of a sacred fire is primarily a symbolic/social role.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to a single cultic institution (perpetual fires).
scores.persistence_reasoning: Long-term role held for the remainder of life once 'past the age of marriage'.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Status of being a widow past childbearing age is biologically/legally fixed.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Plutarch describes historical/traditional Greek religious practices.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: States the custom as an observed factual rule in specific locales.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Widowhood does not inherently include fire-tending duties.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The restriction on marriage has direct material effects on inheritance and household.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific prohibition within a professional/religious role.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Lasts for a thirty-year term, which is a major portion of life.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Occupation of Vestal Virgin is a hard-to-change legal/religious status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Plutarch describes the legendary founding era under Numa.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim cites the ordination/law as an established fact of the order.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The definition of 'Virgin' includes chastity, but the 30-year rule is a specific regulation.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Testamentary freedom and financial autonomy are high-materiality rights.
scores.generality_reasoning: Management of all private affairs without a guardian is a broad legal capacity.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Holds throughout the duration of their thirty-year service.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Vestal status is a long-term, legally formalised social role.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes archaic Roman legal privileges granted by Numa.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Direct description of legal 'privileges' bestowed by the king.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a priestess does not definitionally negate the need for a legal guardian.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Sparing a life is the most tangible material resource possible.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to a specific serendipitous encounter and oath-taking procedure.
scores.persistence_reasoning: A one-off, momentary event with no durable afterlife for the Virgin.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Vestal status is a formal and very hard-to-change legal identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Archaic/Legendary Roman custom described by Plutarch.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the legal consequence of an encounter as factual law.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Granting pardons is not part of the definition of 'Vestal Virgin'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Scourging on bare flesh is a direct material bodily violation.
scores.generality_reasoning: Applies only to 'minor offences' within the specific order.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The punishment is a brief episode, though traumatic.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Vestal status is a stable and formal legal/religious identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes ancient/traditional Roman disciplinary practices.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim describes the procedure of punishment as reported fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Scourging is not definitionally part of being a religious official.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Loss of life (capital punishment) is the ultimate material deprivation.
scores.generality_reasoning: Linked to one specific violation (chastity) for one specific group.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Death is a permanent, structural outcome.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Vestal status is a formal, government-regulated social role.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the historical penal code for the Vestal Virgins.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Provides a factual description of the execution method and law.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The definition of 'Vestal' does not inherently include being buried alive.
